meta.title: MergeCore Swift Rules
meta.description: Rules for Swift code: optionals, concurrency, memory cycles, errors, and idiomatic value types.
meta.notes: Gate with swift: true. Assumes Swift 5.9+; flag version-specific patterns explicitly.

scoring.normalisation: Per review unit (one .swift file), sum penalties after optional quality_signal multiplier, then score = max(0, min(10, initial_score - min(sum_penalties, max_total_penalty_per_file))).

interpretation.9_to_10: Safe optionals, clear actor boundaries, no obvious retain cycles.
interpretation.7_to_8: Mergeable; tighten a capture list or narrow a force cast.
interpretation.5_to_6: Risky: force unwraps on data paths or sync work on the main actor.
interpretation.0_to_4: Block: crashes, data races, or unchecked continuations.

rules.title: Force unwrap on a non-literal value
rules.description: Using ! on a value that can be nil at runtime crashes the app. Use if-let, guard-let, nil coalescing, or meaningful error paths.

rules.title: Force cast with as!
rules.description: as! crashes on mismatch. Prefer as? with a guard or a typed API that removes the need to cast.

rules.title: Closure captures self strongly in an escaping context
rules.description: Escaping closures on view controllers, cancellables, or Tasks should capture self weakly or unowned to avoid retain cycles.

rules.title: Unstructured Task without cancellation handling
rules.description: Task { } started during view or object lifetime should be stored and cancelled when the owner goes away; otherwise work continues after dismissal.

rules.title: try! used on a throwing call
rules.description: try! crashes on error and hides failures. Prefer do/try/catch, try?, or bubble the error up.

rules.title: Blocking work on the MainActor
rules.description: Heavy synchronous work inside @MainActor or on DispatchQueue.main stalls the UI. Move to background queues, actors, or Task.detached.

rules.title: Class used where struct would suffice
rules.description: Value-type models (struct) give immutability and thread-safety by default. Reach for class only when reference semantics are required.

heuristics: No identity requirement, no ObjC bridging

rules.title: Completion-handler callback without Result or error propagation
rules.description: Callbacks that pass (value, error) separately confuse callers. Prefer async/await or Result<Success, Failure>.

rules.title: Sensitive data stored in UserDefaults
rules.description: Tokens, passwords, and PII should use the Keychain. UserDefaults is unencrypted and syncs in some configurations.

rules.title: Strong pattern: actor for mutable shared state
rules.description: Using actor to isolate mutable state gives compile-time safety against data races.

rules.title: Singleton used where dependency injection would help
rules.description: Widespread .shared singletons make tests brittle. Inject protocol-typed dependencies for units that need to be mocked.

heuristics: Used inside business logic rather than app composition

rules.title: print used for production logging
rules.description: print has no levels or subsystems. Use os.Logger (or OSLog) for structured, filterable logs.
